title: Dual-maintenance surface: Pest Control tool list in two files
evidence: Both locations continue to list the tool set independently with no canonical designation or cross-reference. checkpoints.md:L17 reads: 'Pest Control has tools: Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep (no Task tool).' README.md:L307 reads: 'pest-control | Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep |'. The fix requested (designate one location canonical, add cross-reference in the other) has not been applied. The two surfaces are currently consistent but the structural drift risk remains.
priority_check: P3 priority is appropriate. The issue is a documentation hygiene risk — no immediate breakage, but future edits to either file could silently desync the tool lists.
notes: Has description. Both file locations verified by direct read. README.md:L306-307 and checkpoints.md:L17 both read independently with no pointer to each other.

title: CCB reconciliation formula does not detect orphaned merge targets in dedup log
evidence: CCB Check 1 (checkpoints.md:L533-536) requires 'every finding must be accounted for — either standalone, merged into a group, or explicitly marked as duplicate in the deduplication log.' CCB Check 6 (L566-571) spot-checks that merged groups share common files or functions. Neither check addresses the orphaned merge target scenario: if finding A is merged into root-cause group G, and group G is itself listed as a duplicate of group G2, then A is orphaned — it traces only to G, which is itself a duplicate entry. The reconciliation math in Check 1 would still balance (G is counted once) but A has no real bead. No guard for this edge case exists in the current CCB section.
priority_check: P3 priority is appropriate. This is an edge-case gap in a verification step — it would only surface in sessions with complex cross-cutting root-cause merges where consolidation is particularly aggressive.
notes: No description in bead record. Verdict inferred from title. The orphaned merge target scenario is a specific gap in the Check 1 counting formula combined with Check 6's spot-check scope.

title: fix: document artifact naming convention transition point for historical sessions
evidence: The artifact naming conventions section (checkpoints.md:L26-33) documents only the current convention (pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md and pc-session-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md with YYYYMMDD-HHmmss timestamp format). No note acknowledges that sessions prior to _session-068ecc83 used wave-based naming (e.g., 'pc-session-cco-impl-wave1.md', 'pc-session-wwd-wave2.md') or mixed conventions. The acceptance criteria — 'checkpoints.md acknowledges historical naming variation', 'Transition point documented', 'Note clarifies historical artifacts are expected to diverge' — are all unmet.
priority_check: P2 priority is appropriate. Anyone auditing or recovering from a historical session would find artifacts that do not match the documented convention, causing confusion about compliance. This is a documentation gap with real operational impact.
notes: Has detailed description. checkpoints.md:L26-33 verified by direct read. The DRIFT-019 audit reference in the description supports the P2 classification.

title: CCB Check 7 O(N) bead scan not scoped to session-created beads
evidence: checkpoints.md:L574 reads: 'Run `bd list --status=open --after={SESSION_START_DATE}` and cross-reference against the consolidated summary's Beads filed list.' The `--after={SESSION_START_DATE}` parameter explicitly scopes the scan to session-created beads. The accompanying NOTE at L575-577 confirms intent: '{SESSION_START_DATE}: the Queen-supplied session start date (ISO 8601 format, e.g., 2026-02-20). This scopes results to beads filed during this session only and prevents pulling thousands of unrelated open beads from earlier sessions.' The O(N) unscoped scan this bead described no longer exists in the current file.
priority_check: P3 priority was appropriate when filed. The fix is present and the guard note is explicit about its purpose.
notes: No description in bead record. Verdict inferred from title. Bead filed 2026-02-20; the scoping parameter and explanatory note are present in the current file. This was likely added as part of the Review Pipeline Polish epic (ant-farm-qp1j) work.

title: Verification Pipeline no defined precedence when Big Head and CCB checks disagree
evidence: A full read of checkpoints.md finds no precedence rule between Big Head and CCB. The Verdict Thresholds Summary (L49-104) defines each checkpoint's verdict states independently. The CCB section (L497-603) defines its 8 checks and verdict levels without referencing what happens when CCB's verdict conflicts with Big Head's consolidated report. The Queen's Response section for CCB (L595-603) describes PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL handling but says nothing about resolving disagreements with Big Head. No cross-checkpoint tie-breaking rule exists anywhere in the file.
priority_check: P3 priority is appropriate. The gap would only manifest in practice if Big Head consolidates a PASS-worthy result but CCB flags an integrity failure (or vice versa). The current process implicitly treats CCB as authoritative (CCB MUST PASS before presenting results), but this is not stated as a precedence rule.
notes: No description in bead record. Verdict inferred from title. The implicit CCB authority (L498: 'CCB must PASS before presenting results to the user') could be interpreted as a de facto precedence rule, but it is not framed as resolving Big Head vs CCB disagreement — it only gates user presentation.

title: bd show failure guard only on DMVDC Check 2, missing from other bd show callsites
evidence: The DMVDC Check 2 guard (the fix that triggered this bead) is present at checkpoints.md:L363-368: 'GUARD: bd show Failure Handling (INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE)' with full fallback instructions. SSV Check 2 (L644-650) and SSV Check 3 (L665) also have guards. However, two callsites still lack guards: (1) WWD prompt at L277 ('Expected files (from `bd show {TASK_ID}`)') and L294 ('PASS verdict: All changed files are in the expected scope (from `bd show {TASK_ID}`)') — no failure handling block follows either reference. (2) CCB Check 2 at L539 ('For each bead ID in the consolidated summary, run `bd show <id>`') — no guard block follows. The bead's scope (WWD and CCB Check 2 callsites) remains unaddressed.
priority_check: P3 priority is appropriate. The missing guards mean a bd infrastructure failure during WWD or CCB Check 2 would not be gracefully handled, but the pipeline would likely surface an error rather than silently produce wrong results.
notes: Has description. All bd show occurrences verified via grep across the full 712-line file. Lines 277, 294 (WWD) and 539 (CCB Check 2) confirmed to lack GUARD blocks. Lines 363-368 (DMVDC), 644-650 (SSV Check 2), 665 (SSV Check 3) confirmed to have guards.
